setting.note: Lily Bart falls through New York one address at a time. The novel opens in the crowd at Grand Central and follows her down: the bachelor flats of the Benedick, the Trenors' great estate up the Hudson, the marble mansions creeping up Fifth Avenue, and, at the end, a milliner's workroom, a West Side boarding-house, and a bench in Bryant Park. The plot is a map, read downwards.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Edith Wharton, first published 1905; the author died in 1937.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1905), and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1937).
rights.attribution: Edith Wharton, The House of Mirth (1905). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The crimson of the plush drawing-rooms Lily needs and cannot keep. A colour drawn straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

selden.name: Lawrence Selden
selden.colorSourceQuote: moon rose in the denser blue
